pattern: Modified binary search on a rotated array works even with duplicates by shrinking the window when the endpoints tie obscures which half is sorted.
hints: A rotated sorted array always has at least one half around the midpoint that is still in increasing order, which tells you where the target can be.
Duplicates break the usual test when the low, mid, and high values are equal, because you cannot tell which side is sorted.
When nums[lo] == nums[mid] == nums[hi], just step both ends inward by one; otherwise decide the sorted half and discard the half that cannot contain the target.

edge_inputs.description: target present after rotation with duplicates
many leading duplicates hide the pivot

stress.description: sorted array with duplicates rotated at a random pivot, mixed hit and miss targets
stress.note: small value range guarantees heavy duplication
